title: The feeling acknowledged, the feeling at rest
subtitle: A short bedtime visualisation for putting the emotional weight of the day down before sleep.
excerpt: A short visualisation for Day 6 of the SLEEP program. You see the feeling that has been sitting in your body, acknowledge it plainly, and watch it settle. Nothing is fixed; everything is set down.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Developed as a companion practice to Day 6 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

content.text: A short visualisation for Day 6 of the SLEEP program: acknowledging what is there, then setting it down. The practice doesn't require the feeling to disappear, only to be given enough space to stop asking for attention.
Lie down and close your eyes. Take two slow breaths.
Bring to mind the feeling that is present, the one that has been sitting in your chest or stomach since the day. You don't need to name what caused it. Just notice where it lives in your body.
Imagine yourself turning toward it, the way you might turn toward a person waiting for you to notice them. You say, simply: I see you. I hear you. I'm not asking you to leave.
Watch what happens when the feeling is acknowledged rather than pushed away. Notice whether it shifts at all, softer around the edges, smaller, or simply less urgent.
Picture yourself setting it down beside the bed, not throwing it away, not solving it. Just placing it somewhere outside your body for the night. It will be there if you need it in the morning.
Let your body feel the relief of not having to carry it into sleep.
Original to homemade.education. Developed as a companion practice to Day 6 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
